'''Dan Android''' was a [[Gender/Legends|masculine]]<ref name="Star Tours" /> [[Public relations droid|PR droid]]<ref name="Complete Guide to Star Tours" /> with [[Color/Legends|blue]] [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]].<ref name="Star Tours" /> By the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[4 ABY/Legends|4.3 ABY]],<ref name="KOTHB">{{Blog|holocron|7|Death Star timeline}}</ref> he was in charge of public relations for the [[Star Tours/Legends|Star Tours]] travel agency,<ref name="Star Tours" /> introducing other tours on [[Tourscan]].<ref name="Complete Guide to Star Tours" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|PR droid for Star Tours seen on Tourscan. May be distantly related to movie star Dan Aykroyd.|Dan Android's description in ''Complete Guide to Star Tours''|<ref name="Complete Guide to Star Tours" />}}
Dan Android was created for [[Tokyo Disneyland]]'s [[Wikipedia:Simulator ride|simulator ride]] [[Wikipedia:Amusement park|theme park]] attraction [[Star Tours (real-world)|''Star Tours'']], which opened on [[July 12]], [[1989]]. He was named after actor [[Wikipedia:Dan Aykroyd|Dan Aykroyd]], which was jokingly referenced by a Spot the Droid poster.<ref name="Star Tours" />

The locales that Dan Android advertised on the Tourscan<ref name="Star Tours" /> were actually recycled footage from [[Wikipedia:Horizons (Epcot)|Horizons]], a now-defunct [[Wikipedia:Epcot|Epcot]] ride.<ref name="Epcot">{{WebCite|url=http://progresscityusa.com/2011/09/01/the-horizons-story-part-iii-disneys-house-of-magic|text=The Horizons Story, Part III: Disney's House Of Magic|author=Michael Crawford|date=2011-09-24|work=The Horizons Story|archivedate=20110924203238}}</ref>

*[[Star Tours (real-world)|''Star Tours'']] {{1st}} {{C|Tokyo only}}
